name: Demographic Winter — Global Sub-Replacement Fertility
description: Sub-replacement fertility applied as a sustained global drag over an 80-year horizon. As total fertility rates settle below 2.1 worldwide, shrinking and ageing workforces erode growth everywhere — but large, already-mature economies feel it first and hardest as their dependency ratios climb. Younger, higher-fertility economies decline more slowly and gradually inherit a larger share of global output. A slow-motion structural shift rather than a shock. Tested against the built-in GDP demo.
author: data-isc
